title: Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement

source.name: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

summary: On March 17, 2021, the Federal Reserve unanimously voted to maintain the federal funds rate target range at 0 to 1/4 percent and continue asset purchases at $80 billion per month in Treasury securities and $40 billion per month in agency mortgage-backed securities, with no changes to its operational framework or rate on reserve balances.
key_points: Board of Governors voted unanimously to maintain the interest rate paid on required and excess reserve balances at 0.10 percent, effective March 18, 2021
Federal funds rate maintained in target range of 0 to 1/4 percent
Desk directed to increase Treasury holdings by $80 billion/month and agency MBS by $40 billion/month
Overnight reverse repurchase agreement operations maintained at 0.00 percent offering rate with $80 billion/day per-counterparty limit
Primary credit rate approved at existing level of 0.25 percent
All principal payments from agency debt and agency MBS to be reinvested in agency MBS
why_it_matters: This implementation note confirms the Fed maintained its highly accommodative monetary policy stance amid ongoing economic recovery from the pandemic. The unchanged asset purchase pace and near-zero rate target signal continued support for market liquidity and borrowing conditions.
macro_context: The maintenance of current policy settings indicates the Fed viewed economic conditions as not yet warranting a shift in either direction. The $120 billion monthly QE pace and zero lower bound rate provide substantial monetary accommodation supporting economic recovery.
market_structure_context: The implementation details specify operational parameters including $80 billion/day RRP counterparty limit, the reinvestment policy for agency debt/MBS principal payments, and authorization for agency CMBS purchases to support market functioning.
notes: Historical FOMC implementation note from March 2021. Source is a primary Federal Reserve document with high factual accuracy. While the document is well-structured and contains explicit policy details, it is dated and represents a past policy meeting rather than current or recent policy. The content confirms policy maintenance as expected from the accompanying FOMC statement.
